# Bitsocial Docs

> Bitsocial Docs is the technical documentation surface for the Bitsocial protocol, roadmap, apps, anti-spam modules, infrastructure, and contributor workflows.

Use this file as the short routing index for `https://docs.bitsocial.net/`. Use `https://docs.bitsocial.net/llms-full.txt` when you want the full inline documentation corpus instead of the curated map.

## Related landing page context

- [Home](https://bitsocial.net/): Bitsocial is an open-source peer-to-peer network for social apps, with no servers, no global bans, where users and communities are cryptographic property.
- [Core features](https://bitsocial.net/): Bitsocial is open source, peer-to-peer via IPFS/libp2p, app-oriented, serverless by default, locally moderated with no protocol-level global bans, and built around key-controlled identities and communities.
- [Sanctuary comparison](https://bitsocial.net/#decentralized): Compares Server-based (Bluesky, Mastodon, Lemmy); Blockchain-based (Farcaster, Lens, DeSo, Steemit); Bitsocial (Pure P2P with arbitrary anti-spam challenges) across self-hosting cost, who keeps content online, scaling, custom anti-spam logic, and takedown choke points.
- [Deep comparison tables](https://bitsocial.net/#nostr-comparison): Sourced modal tables compare Bitsocial with Nostr, Bluesky, ActivityPub / Fediverse, Farcaster, Lens, DeSo, Steemit across Spam replies, Community rules, Scaling costs, Data hosting, Censorship points, Who accepts posts?, First post, Human names, Finding content.
- [Arbitrary Challenges](https://bitsocial.net/#arbitrary-challenges): Each node runs its own anti-spam challenge, exchanged purely peer-to-peer with the user. When spam evolves, nodes adapt without changing the protocol. Example modules include any code, AI-powered moderation, SMS OTP, whatever comes next.
- [Text-only Protocol](https://bitsocial.net/#text-only-protocol): There's no global admin and no server to seize, so nobody can ban you from the whole network. Moderation just moves closer to you.
- [Master Plan](https://bitsocial.net/#master-plan): Bitsocial wins by attracting as many builders as possible. Run your own unstoppable community, develop your own decentralized social app, or even launch your own business on top of Bitsocial. The tide rises with every builder.

## Protocol notes

- [Custom Anti-Spam Challenges](https://docs.bitsocial.net/custom-challenges/): Why Bitsocial lets each community define its own anti-spam policy.
- [Identity and Community Ownership](https://docs.bitsocial.net/identity-and-ownership/): How Bitsocial treats profiles and communities as key-controlled property instead of revocable platform accounts.
- [Local Moderation, Not Global Bans](https://docs.bitsocial.net/local-moderation/): What Bitsocial means when it says moderation stays local.
- [Peer-to-Peer Protocol](https://docs.bitsocial.net/peer-to-peer-protocol/): How Bitsocial uses IPFS/libp2p, public-key addressing, peer-to-peer pubsub, and browser P2P nodes to deliver serverless social media.

## Master plan

- [Bitsocial Chain](https://docs.bitsocial.net/bitsocial-network/): Phase 2 of the master plan, covering the proposed Ethereum L2 appchain economic layer for Bitsocial apps.
- [Build your own Bitsocial client](https://docs.bitsocial.net/build-your-own-client/): Builder guide for shipping independent Bitsocial clients, from imageboards and forums to niche social apps.
- [Decentralize All Social Media](https://docs.bitsocial.net/decentralize-all-social-media/): Phase 5 of the master plan, focused on funding and building the long tail of Bitsocial social clients.
- [Decentralize Twitter/X](https://docs.bitsocial.net/flagship-bitsocial-app/): Phase 3 of the master plan: a focused, decentralized Twitter/X alternative for text-first public conversation with replaceable infrastructure.
- [Scale Bitsocial Economies](https://docs.bitsocial.net/scale-bitsocial-economies/): Phase 4 of the master plan, focused on funding, infrastructure pluralism, and competing services around Bitsocial.

## Apps

- [5chan](https://docs.bitsocial.net/apps/5chan/): A serverless, decentralized imageboard built on the Bitsocial protocol where anyone can create and own boards.
- [Seedit](https://docs.bitsocial.net/apps/seedit/): A serverless, decentralized Reddit-style forum built on the Bitsocial protocol where anyone can create and own communities.

## Developer tools

- [Bitsocial CLI](https://docs.bitsocial.net/developer-tools/cli/): Command-line interface for running a Bitsocial node, creating communities, and managing protocol operations.
- [React Hooks](https://docs.bitsocial.net/developer-tools/react-hooks/): React hooks library for building decentralized social applications on the Bitsocial protocol.

## Anti-spam challenges

- [Captcha Canvas Challenge](https://docs.bitsocial.net/anti-spam/captcha-canvas-challenge/): Standalone image-based captcha challenge for Bitsocial communities.
- [EVM Contract Call Challenge](https://docs.bitsocial.net/anti-spam/evm-contract-call/): Anti-spam challenge that verifies on-chain conditions by calling an EVM smart contract.
- [Spam Blocker](https://docs.bitsocial.net/anti-spam/spam-blocker/): Centralized spam detection service with risk scoring, OAuth challenges, and configurable tier thresholds.
- [Voucher Challenge](https://docs.bitsocial.net/anti-spam/voucher-challenge/): Anti-spam challenge that gates publishing behind unique voucher codes distributed by community owners.

## Infrastructure

- [BSO Resolver](https://docs.bitsocial.net/infrastructure/bso-resolver/): Resolve .bso domain names to public keys via Bitsocial TXT records.
- [Mintpass](https://docs.bitsocial.net/infrastructure/mintpass/): NFT-based authentication system that helps Bitsocial communities verify users and reduce sybil attacks.
- [Pubsub Provider](https://docs.bitsocial.net/infrastructure/pubsub-provider/): Fallback pubsub relay and delegated routing provider for Bitsocial operators.
- [Telegram Bots](https://docs.bitsocial.net/infrastructure/telegram-bots/): Feed bots that monitor Bitsocial community lists and forward posts to Telegram channels.

## Contributor playbooks

- [Bug Investigation Workflow](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/bug-investigation/): Use this when a bug is reported in a specific file/line/code block.
- [Commit and Issue Format](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/commit-issue-format/): Use this when proposing or implementing meaningful code changes.
- [Agent Hooks Setup](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/hooks-setup/): If your AI coding assistant supports lifecycle hooks, configure these for this repo.
- [Known Surprises](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/known-surprises/): This file tracks repository specific confusion points that caused agent mistakes.
- [Long-Running Agent Workflow](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/long-running-agent-workflow/): Use this playbook when a task is likely to span multiple sessions, handoffs, or spawned agents.
- [Skills and Tools](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/skills-and-tools/): Use this playbook when setting up/adjusting skills and external tooling.
- [Feature List Template](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/templates/feature-list-template/): Machine-readable template used for long-running multi-session task tracking.
- [Progress Log](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/templates/progress.template/): Append one entry per session.
- [Translations Workflow](https://docs.bitsocial.net/agent-playbooks/translations/): This project uses i18next translation files in public/translations/{lang}/default.json.

## Other

- [Content Discovery](https://docs.bitsocial.net/content-discovery/): How Bitsocial separates peer discovery from app-level curation.
- [i18n Default Language Policy](https://docs.bitsocial.net/i18n-default-language-policy/): Anonymous visitors resolve language in this order:
- [A complete layman explanation of the Bitsocial protocol](https://docs.bitsocial.net/layman-protocol-explanation/): A plain-English walkthrough of Bitsocial communities, peer lookup, publishing, anti-spam challenges, moderation, and apps.

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://docs.bitsocial.net/llms-full.txt): Full inline markdown aggregation of the public Bitsocial docs plus companion project READMEs.
- [Main site llms.txt](https://bitsocial.net/llms.txt): Site-wide entry point covering the public web surfaces outside the docs app.
- [Main site llms-full.txt](https://bitsocial.net/llms-full.txt): Site-wide full corpus that includes the public docs and the app/project README appendix.
